title: Subagents, Parallelism, and Coordination · Fault-Injection Lab
summary: Coordinate specialist agents without duplicate or conflicting work. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a bounded multi-agent execution map with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Delegate only concrete independent work with a clear return contract.
Give a subagent the minimum context required without losing governing constraints.
Coordinate shared files, locks, messages, and ownership boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
